data.title: STOCKS | Deutsche Bank Predicts Seasonal Boost for US Equities Amid Uncertain Crypto Market
data.description: Deutsche Bank anticipates a seasonal uplift for U.S. equities, driven by an influx of $11 billion in tax refunds returning to households. According to NS3.AI, while this development may positively impact the stock market, the outlook for the cryptocurrency market remains uncertain. Bitcoin is currently facing challenges in maintaining its gains, with predictions of potential further declines. Investors are exhibiting cautious sentiment, as they await possible Federal Reserve rate cuts that could enhance the appeal of riskier assets such as cryptocurrencies.

data.title: England's Housebuilding Set to Hit Decade Low, Challenging Starmer's Housing Goals
data.description: Housebuilding in England is projected to decline to its lowest level in over ten years, complicating Prime Minister Keir Starmer's commitment to construct 1.5 million homes. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges facing the housing sector amid economic uncertainties. The anticipated drop in construction activity underscores the difficulties in meeting housing targets, which are crucial for addressing the country's housing shortage. This development raises concerns about the feasibility of achieving the government's ambitious housing plans.

data.title: Emerging Markets Remain Stable Amid Dollar Strength and Holiday Impact
data.description: Emerging-market stocks and bonds showed minimal movement, influenced by a stronger dollar and reduced trading volumes. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that holidays in key markets such as China and South Korea contributed to the subdued activity. The firmer dollar has been a significant factor in limiting changes in these markets, as investors remain cautious amid the holiday period. The lack of significant trading activity reflects the impact of these holidays on market dynamics, with investors awaiting further developments in global economic conditions.

data.title: Analysis: The SOPR for long-term holders has fallen below 1 for the first time since the end of the bear market in 2023.
data.description: Crypto analyst Darkfost wrote on the X platform that long-term holders (LTH) are showing early signs of stress. As the market correction continues, LTH holders are beginning to feel the pressure, according to the SOPR (Spend-to-Profit Ratio). While the annualized LTH SOPR is 1.87, it has fallen below the threshold of 1 to 0.88, the first time this has happened since the end of the bear market in 2023. This suggests that LTH holders are gradually starting to sell at a loss, reflecting increasing market pressure. However, this trend is not yet fully established. Looking at the monthly average, the SOPR remains at 1.09, indicating that most sales are still profitable over a broader timeframe. Therefore, the market has not yet entered a true capitulation phase for LTH holders. These are just early signs of weakening sentiment, which may fade if the market stabilizes, or intensify if selling pressure persists.

data.title: STOCKS | Bank of America Upgrades Albemarle to Buy, Raises Price Target
data.description: Bank of America has upgraded its rating for Albemarle Corporation (ALB.N) from neutral to buy. According to Jin10, the financial institution has also increased its price target for the company from $167.00 to $190.00. This adjustment reflects a more optimistic outlook on Albemarle's market performance.

data.title: Incident Reported Near Yemen's Coast Involving Small Vessel
data.description: An incident has been reported near the coast of Yemen, approximately 70 nautical miles southwest of Aden. According to Jin10, a white small boat approached a ship and signaled with a horn. The boat was carrying five individuals.

data.title: Emirates CFO Michael Doersam to Depart by End of June
data.description: Emirates Chief Financial Officer Michael Doersam is expected to step down from his position by the end of June, according to sources. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that Doersam's departure marks a significant change in the airline's executive team. The reasons for his departure have not been disclosed, and Emirates has yet to comment on the matter. Doersam has been with the company for several years, contributing to its financial strategies and operations. His exit comes at a time when the airline industry is navigating post-pandemic recovery challenges.

data.title: Shiba Inu Faces Challenges Amid Low Network Activity and Token Burn Concerns
data.description: Shiba Inu (SHIB) is trading significantly below its peak in 2021, with growing concerns about the low activity on its Shibarium network and ineffective token burn rates. According to NS3.AI, the daily transaction volumes remain minimal, and the current token burn rate suggests it would take over 1,000 years to substantially reduce the supply. Despite these challenges, the development team is actively working on the project, promising privacy upgrades by 2026. Investor sentiment is divided, largely dependent on the execution of the roadmap and whether Shibarium can achieve meaningful adoption to bolster SHIB's value proposition.

data.title: Cardano Founder Invests $200 Million in Privacy Blockchain Midnight
data.description: Charles Hoskinson, the founder of Cardano, has made a personal investment of $200 million into Midnight, a new blockchain project focused on privacy. According to NS3.AI, this investment strategy deliberately avoids venture capital to ensure the project's independence and uphold user-centric values. Hoskinson emphasized that Midnight is not designed to draw privacy maximalists from existing projects such as Zcash or Monero.

data.title: CryptoQuant: Binance holds $47.5 billion in stablecoins, representing 65% of the exchange's total liquidity.
data.description: CryptoQuant, in an article published on the X platform, stated that $47.5 billion in stablecoin liquidity is currently concentrated on a single exchange. Binance holds 65% of all stablecoin liquidity across exchanges, while competitors lag far behind, even with slower outflows during the bear market. Funds haven't left the crypto space; they're concentrating.

data.title: Vattenfall Plans to Exit French Energy Retail Market
data.description: Swedish utility company Vattenfall is preparing to withdraw from the French energy retail market. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision comes as energy prices decrease and suppliers lose access to discounted power from Electricite de France. The move reflects the challenges faced by energy companies in adapting to changing market conditions.

data.title: 54% of Users Held Stablecoins as Utility Expands Beyond Trading: Global Survey
data.description:
Stablecoins are now being used as practical financial tools like payments, payroll, and savings in addition to trading mediums. The study suggests that adoption trends are strongest in regions where currency volatility and cross-border friction are challenging.
source: https://voiceofcrypto.online/crypto-news/54-of-users-held-stablecoins-as-utility-expands-beyond-trading-global-survey-finds

data.title: Canada's January CPI Anticipated to Match December's Increase
data.description: Canada's Consumer Price Index (CPI) for January is expected to show a 2.4% year-over-year increase, consistent with December's figures. According to NS3.AI, the Bank of Canada is likely to maintain current interest rates, as inflation remains above the target but is showing signs of moderation. Market attention is focused on how these inflation trends and trade-related costs might impact the Canadian Dollar and the USD/CAD exchange rate.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | U.S. to Enhance Missile Defense in the Philippines
data.description: The United States plans to deploy additional missile systems to the Philippines, a decision that may provoke a reaction from China. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strengthening defense relationship between the two nations. This strategic move aims to bolster security in the region amid rising tensions and territorial disputes in the South China Sea. The deployment underscores the commitment of the U.S. and the Philippines to enhance their military cooperation and address regional security challenges.

data.title: France and Germany Urge EU for Simplified Financial Services Plan
data.description: France and Germany have called on the European Commission to expedite the introduction of a comprehensive 'Simplified Financial Services Plan.' According to Jin10, this initiative aims to make EU regulations more comprehensible and reduce the burden on businesses. The proposal reflects a joint effort by the two countries to streamline financial services within the European Union, enhancing clarity and efficiency in regulatory frameworks.

data.title: AI Cloud Computing Investment Raises Concerns for Microsoft and OpenAI
data.description: The $1.6 trillion investment in AI cloud computing is sparking concerns among suppliers such as Microsoft and customers like OpenAI. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential challenges and implications of this substantial financial commitment. The investment aims to enhance AI capabilities, but it also raises questions about the sustainability and impact on both providers and users in the tech industry.

data.title: Russia's Baltic Sea Exports Threatened by Extensive Sea Ice
data.description: Russia's key Baltic Sea commodity ports are experiencing the most extensive sea ice in 15 years, potentially impacting the nation's export program. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns over a shortage of vessels capable of navigating the icy conditions. This development could significantly affect the transportation of goods, as the availability of ice-capable ships is limited. The situation underscores the challenges faced by Russia in maintaining its export levels amid changing environmental conditions.

data.title: Analysts: Number of new crypto funds hits 5-year low; total funds raised from 2023 to 2025 will only be close to the total for 2022.
data.description: Crypto KOL Edgy stated on the X platform that 85% of token issuance projects will be unprofitable in 2025. Venture capital-backed projects are barely breaking even, with some even incurring significant losses. A chart from Galaxy Research shows that in the second quarter of 2022, crypto venture capital firms raised nearly $17 billion through over 80 new funds; however, their investment returns have been declining since 2022, with the number of new funds hitting a five-year low, and last quarter's fundraising amount only 12% of that in the second quarter of 2022. The $8.5 billion invested by venture capital firms last quarter was not new capital, but rather surplus funds raised in 2022. The total capital deployed from 2023 to 2025 is roughly equivalent to the total funds raised in 2022. The model of raising funds, issuing tokens, and dumping them on retail investors is coming to an end. As the influence of venture capital firms diminishes, projects with real users and revenue will prevail, leading to fairer issuance methods, less insider selling, and more developers focusing on product optimization rather than fundraising.

data.title: Genuine Parts Plans to Split Automotive and Industrial Parts Divisions
data.description: Genuine Parts Company has announced plans to separate its automotive and industrial parts divisions. According to Jin10, this strategic move aims to streamline operations and enhance focus on each sector's growth potential. The decision reflects the company's commitment to optimizing its business structure and improving efficiency. Genuine Parts is known for its extensive distribution network and diverse product offerings, and this split is expected to allow for more targeted strategies in both divisions. The company has not yet disclosed specific timelines or further details regarding the separation process.
